title: Sensei Dashboard Agent Handoff V1
description: A bounded, dashboard-populated request handed to a connected agent (Claude, Codex, or another agent) to continue the user's current architectural investigation. This is a separate, mutable, per-interaction envelope, not part of the immutable dashboard-projection-v1 document.
$comment: schema_version is a const: any field addition, removal, rename, or semantic change requires a new schema identifier/version, and a consumer must reject an envelope whose schema_version it does not exactly recognize. The dashboard may populate this envelope only from validated fields of the current dashboard-projection-v1 document plus explicit user input (selection, lens, requested intent); it must not invent, infer, or repair architectural fields when constructing it.

selected_element.$comment: null when the handoff concerns the current view as a whole rather than one selected element.

referenced_ids.description: Stable identifiers already supplied by the current projection that ground this request. All categories are optional lists, not inferred relationships.

active_context.$comment: Same public-snapshot redaction rule as the projection's active_context: 'session' context must never be included, and 'task'/'pull_request' context must be omitted unless every included field has been explicitly marked public. The dashboard does not decide what is safe to publish.

observation_limitations.description: Carried forward from the source projection's availability/coverage limitations so the handoff never implies that visible evidence is exhaustive when observation was partial.

capability.$comment: read_only: the agent may explain, review, retrieve evidence, or compare. propose: the agent may additionally prepare or propose a governed change, but still may not mutate architectural state directly (product contract §3.3). This is distinct from the projection-level capabilities.agent_handoff transport flag (none/export/live), which governs whether a handoff affordance exists at all.
